sections.heading: Power of the Cabinet Secretary to issue rules and regulations ......................................................................................... 15
sections.summary: The Cabinet Secretary has authority to create rules and regulations covering securities listing, disclosures by market participants, record-keeping requirements, capital market operations, foreign investor participation, investment schemes, venture capital, credit rating agencies, securities trading, clearing, settlement, forex activities, fund managers, and financial penalties. These rules must align with promoting an efficient securities market and be publicly exposed for 30 days for stakeholder feedback.
sections.dtia_summary: Rules and regulations must be consistent with market efficiency objectives and undergo public consultation before implementation, which impacts how financial data is governed across borders through the Authority's regulatory framework.

requirements.text: The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate listing and de-listing of securities on a securities exchange
The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate disclosures about securities transactions by stockbrokers, derivatives brokers and dealers

The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate the keeping and proper maintenance of books, records, accounts and audits by all persons approved or licensed by the Authority
All rules and regulations formulated under subsection (1) shall take into account and be consistent with the objective of promoting and maintaining an effective and efficient securities market

All rules and regulations formulated under subsection (1) shall be exposed for comment by stakeholders and the general public for a period of thirty days through notification in at least two daily newspapers of national circulation and the electronic media

sections.source_quote: The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate— (a) listing and de-listing of securities on a securities exchange; (b) disclosures about securities transactions by— (i) stockbrokers, derivatives brokers and dealers; (ii) persons who acquire or dispose of securities or exchange-traded derivatives contracts; (iii) a securities exchange;

sections.heading: Furnishing of information to the Authority .............................................................................................................................. 18
sections.summary: The Authority may require persons to provide information in writing within a specified period. The Authority may not disclose information obtained unless required by court law, except when assisting foreign regulatory bodies under specific conditions involving public interest or regulatory functions.
sections.dtia_summary: Information sharing with foreign regulators requires demonstrating public interest or regulatory function, which affects cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: The Authority or any person officially authorized in that behalf by the Authority may, by notice in writing, require any person to furnish to the Authority or to the authorized person, within such period as is specified in the notice, all such returns or information as specified in such notice

The Authority or any member thereof, or any officer or servant of the Authority, shall not disclose to any person or use any return or information acquired under subsection (1) except for the purpose of achieving the objectives of the Authority unless required to do so by a court of law

The Authority may, where it receives a request from a regulatory body, whether established within or outside Kenya, for assistance in investigating a person specified by the regulatory body who has contravened or is contravening any legal or regulatory requirements
A regulatory body which requests for assistance under subsection (3) shall demonstrate that it is desirable or expedient that the assistance requested should be provided in the interest of the public
A regulatory body which requests for assistance under subsection (3) shall demonstrate that the assistance shall assist the regulatory body in the discharge and performance of its functions

The Authority shall, in deciding whether the requirement under subsection (5) is satisfied in a particular case, take into account whether the regulatory body shall pay the Authority any of the costs and expenses incurred in providing the assistance

The Authority shall, in deciding whether the requirement under subsection (5) is satisfied in a particular case, take into account whether the regulatory body shall be able and willing to provide reciprocal assistance within its jurisdiction in response to a similar request for assistance from Kenya

sections.source_quote: The Authority or any person officially authorized in that behalf by the Authority may, by notice in writing, require any person to furnish to the Authority or to the authorized person, within such period as is specified in the notice, all such returns or information as specified in such notice.

sections.heading: Establishment of the Investor Compensation Fund ............................................................................................................... 21
sections.summary: This section establishes the Investor Compensation Fund to compensate investors who suffer pecuniary loss due to licensed stockbrokers or dealers failing to meet contractual obligations. The Fund is funded through contributions from licensed persons, fines, penalties, interest, recovered sums, and approved external sources. The Authority may reward individuals who provide timely information leading to Fund recoveries, with rewards capped at five million shillings. Offenders who collude or provide false information face fines up to five million shillings or imprisonment for five years. This section establishes the Investor Compensation Fund, requiring auditors to prepare and audit financial statements in accordance with regulatory standards. It mandates auditors to report specific adverse changes, potential contraventions of the Act, financial crimes, or serious irregularities to the Authority. The section explicitly states that such reports do not constitute auditor breaches of duty.
sections.dtia_summary: This section creates a financial safeguard mechanism for investor compensation but does not directly address data transfer or processing requirements. It is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments as it focuses on financial compensation mechanisms rather than data flows. This section is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments as it pertains to investor compensation funds and auditor reporting obligations unrelated to data transfers, international processing, or cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: There shall be established a Fund to be known as the Investor Compensation Fund for the purposes of granting compensation to investors who suffer pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed stockbroker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations.

The Compensation Fund shall consist of—(a) such moneys as are required to be paid into the Compensation Fund by licensed persons; (b) such sums of money as are paid under this Act as fines or penalties or under section 34 as ill-gotten gains where those harmed are not specifically identifiable; (c) such sums of money as accrue from interest and profits from investing Compensation Fund moneys; (d) such sums of money recovered by or on behalf of the Authority from entities whose failure to meet their obligations to investors result in payments from the Compensation Fund; (e) interest deemed to accrue on the proceeds of a public issue or offer for sale of shares of a company listed or to be listed on an approved securities exchange, between the closing date and the date of dispatch of refund cheques, or, where there is no refund, the date of dispatch of share certificates or crediting of securities accounts, to be determined at the rate prescribed by the Authority; (f) such sums of money as are received for purposes of the Compensation Fund from any other source approved by the Cabinet Secretary.

The Authority may reward any person who provides new and timely information leading to the recovery of sums of money referred to in subsection (2): Provided that—(a) this provision shall not apply to any officer of the Authority; (b) the reward payable under this subsection shall be three per cent of the amount recovered subject to a maximum of five million shillings; and (c) the reward referred to in paragraph (a) shall be paid before the recovered sums of money are transferred to the Fund.

A person who—(a) colludes with an officer of the Authority for the purpose of collecting the reward under subsection (2A); (b) while working at the Authority aids another person to get and provide information under subsection (2A); (c) provides false information under subsection (2A), commits an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding five million shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years.
financial statements prepared and audited in accordance with the accounts and audit requirements for regulated persons
such other requirements as may be specified by the Authority

An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that there is or has been an adverse change in the risks inherent in the business of a self-regulatory organization with the potential to jeopardize the ability of the self-regulatory organization to continue as a going concern
An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that the self-regulatory organization may be in contravention of any provisions of this Act, or directions issued by the Authority
An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that a financial crime has been or is likely to be committed
An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that serious irregularities have occurred
shall report the matter, in writing, to the Authority

sections.source_quote: 18. Establishment of the Investor Compensation Fund (1) There shall be established a Fund to be known as the Investor Compensation Fund for the purposes of granting compensation to investors who suffer pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed stockbroker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations.

sections.heading: Disciplinary action by securities exchange ............................................................................................................................... 27
sections.summary: A securities exchange or derivatives exchange must notify the Authority within seven days when taking disciplinary action against a trading participant or listed company. The Authority may review such actions, affirm or set aside decisions after providing opportunities for hearing. The Authority can also suspend or expel individuals directly if an exchange fails to act, following similar hearing procedures.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires exchanges to report disciplinary actions to the regulator, enabling oversight of cross-border compliance. The regulator's power to review and override exchange decisions ensures accountability in disciplinary processes, which impacts data transfer assessments involving exchange participants.

requirements.text: A securities exchange or derivatives exchange must notify the Authority within seven days when taking disciplinary action against a trading participant or listed company.
The Authority may review disciplinary actions taken by exchanges and affirm or set aside decisions after providing opportunities for hearing.
The Authority can suspend or expel individuals directly if an exchange fails to act, following similar hearing procedures.

sections.source_quote: Where a securities exchange or a derivatives exchange reprimands, fines, suspends or expels, or otherwise takes disciplinary action against a trading participant or a listed company, it shall within seven days give notice to the Authority in writing, giving particulars including the name of the person, the reason for and nature of the action taken.

sections.heading: Procedure for collective investment schemes .......................................................................................................................... 38
sections.summary: [extraction failed: model output unparseable] This section outlines the procedural requirements for collective investment schemes involving asset-backed securities. It specifies that assets must be legally originated, sold, transferred, or assigned, and comply with the Act. The section details the origination of assets into securitisation trusts, the requirement for true sales according to Kenyan law, and the legal nature of transfers to avoid equitable treatment. It also mandates that failures to achieve true sales do not invalidate securities offerings or adversely affect investor rights. This section outlines the procedural requirements for collective investment schemes involving restricted offers, including the authority's power to apply court procedures for unfair prejudice and require records in cases of restricted offers. It specifies that these procedures mirror those for listed asset-backed securities.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes conditions for asset-backed securities transactions to ensure legal transfers and true sales, which are critical for DTIA assessments to verify compliance with cross-border transfer mechanisms and trust structures in securitisation.

requirements.text: Assets must be capable of being legally originated, sold, transferred or assigned
Assets must comply with any requirements imposed under this Act

All securitisation transactions shall involve either direct origination of assets into the securitisation trust or sale, transfer or assignment of assets to the trustee in a true sale according to Kenyan law
The sale, transfer or assignment must be a legal and not an equitable sale
Failure to achieve a true sale shall not invalidate the sale, transfer or assignment, the issue or offer of asset backed securities, or adversely affect investor rights

The powers of the Authority to make an application to court in case of unfair prejudice under section 33C; or require production of records and documents under section 33D shall apply to restricted offers in the same manner as if the asset backed securities had been listed.

sections.source_quote: a third party at the time at which an issue or offer of asset backed securities is made; be capable of being legally originated, sold, transferred or assigned; and comply with any requirements imposed under this Act.

sections.heading: Register of interest in securities ................................................................................................................................................... 53
sections.summary: [extraction failed: model output unparseable] This section imposes penalties for unauthorized gains or losses from securities transactions. A licensed entity that engages in such activities faces a fine up to ten million shillings and must pay twice the amount of the gain or loss avoided. The penalty applies to entities that contravene the Act's provisions regarding securities transactions. This section mandates that the Authority may issue directions requiring companies or persons to produce specific records and documents related to misconduct, inadequate information disclosure, or other specified matters. It outlines the Authority's power to authorize individuals to compel production of records, and specifies that banks are limited to making copies or extracts of documents. The section also establishes that contravening these requirements constitutes an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIAs by requiring detailed record production for investigations into misconduct or information gaps, with specific limitations on bank records. It may necessitate data sharing with regulators for compliance checks.

requirements.text: A fine not exceeding ten million shillings and payment of twice the amount of the gain made or loss avoided
The Authority may issue directions requiring a company or person to produce records and documents related to misconduct, inadequate information disclosure, or other specified matters
The Authority may authorize a person to require a company or other person to produce records and documents specified by the authorized person
The Authority or authorized person may require a person in possession of records to produce them to the Authority or authorized person
The power to require records or documents includes taking copies or extracts, demanding explanations from officers or employees, or requiring explanations for failure to produce records
For banks, the power is limited to making copies or extracts of documents
Contravening this section constitutes an offence

sections.source_quote: company, to a fine not exceeding ten million shillings and payment of twice the amount of the gain made or loss avoided.

sections.heading: Exemption from Cap. 446 ................................................................................................................................................................ 68
sections.summary: This section exempts certain entities from the application of Cap. 446, which is likely a reference to another act or regulation. It specifies that specific exemptions are granted under the Capital Markets Act (Cap. 485A) to avoid overlapping or conflicting provisions. The exemption applies to entities that would otherwise be subject to Cap. 446, but the exact scope and conditions of the exemption are not detailed in this section.

requirements.text: Exemption from Cap. 446

sections.source_quote: Exemption from Cap. 446

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used throughout the Capital Markets Act. It specifies that 'agent' refers to a person appointed in writing by a licensed person to perform functions on their behalf, excluding derivatives markets. 'Authorised securities dealer' denotes a person authorized to deal in securities in specific market segments. 'Authority' refers to the Capital Markets Authority established by section 5. 'Beneficial owner' identifies the ultimate owner or controller of a legal person or arrangement. 'Capital market instrument' covers long-term financial instruments traded for investment purposes. The section also defines 'collective investment scheme', 'commodity', 'commodity contract', 'commodity market', 'company', 'Compensation Fund', 'credit rating agency', 'dealer', 'dealing in securities', 'derivatives exchange', 'derivatives dealer', 'derivatives market', 'director', 'exchange-traded derivative contracts', 'expert', 'financial instrument', 'fund manager', 'futures broker', 'futures member', 'incorporation documents', 'information memorandum', 'insider', 'investment adviser', 'investment bank', 'investment company', 'key personnel', 'licensed person', and 'mutual fund
sections.dtia_summary: These definitions establish the scope of data subjects and entities that may be subject to data transfer restrictions under the Act, particularly for financial data involving licensed persons and sophisticated investors.

requirements.text: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires—

key personnel means a person who manages or controls the activities of a licensed or a regulated person and includes—(a) the chief executive officer, chief financial officer, chief compliance officer, secretary to the Board, chief internal auditor, or any manager of licensed persons; and (b) any person who holds a position or discharges responsibilities of any person referred to in paragraph (a)
licensed person means a person or body corporate who has been issued with a licence or approved by the Authority

securities means—(a) shares in the share capital of a company; (b) any instrument creating or acknowledging indebtedness which is issued or proposed to be issued; (c) loan stock, bonds and other instruments creating or acknowledging indebtedness by or on behalf of the Government, Central Bank, or public authority; (d) rights, options, or interests, whether described as units or otherwise, in, or in respect of such shares, debt securities and Government and public securities; (e) any right, whether conferred by warrant or otherwise, to subscribe for shares or debt securities; (f) any option to acquire or dispose of any other security; (g) futures in respect of securities or other assets or property; (h) securities and collective investment scheme products structured in conformity with Islamic principles for investments; (i) units in a collective investment scheme, including shares in an investment company, or other similar entities whether established in Kenya or not

sophisticated investor means—(a) a person who is licensed under this Act; (b) an authorized scheme or a collective investment scheme; (c) a bank, a subsidiary of a bank, insurance company, co-operative society, statutory fund, pension or retirement fund; or (d) an individual, company, partnership, association or a trustee on behalf of a trust which, either alone or with any associates on a joint account subscribes for securities with an issue price as the Authority may prescribe from time to time

sections.source_quote: Capital Markets Act (Cap. 485A) Kenya

sections.heading: Definition of "interest in securities"
sections.summary: This section defines 'interest in securities' as a legal concept where a person is deemed to have an interest in securities under specific circumstances, such as holding securities in trust, being a controlling shareholder, having contractual rights to purchase securities, or being entitled to exercise rights attached to securities. It also specifies exceptions where such an interest is disregarded, including bare trusteeship, lending of money, prescribed offices, and certain remote interests.

requirements.text: Where any property held in trust consists of or includes securities in which a person knows, or has reasonable grounds for believing, that he or she has an interest, he or she shall be deemed to have an interest in those securities.

A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security where a body corporate has an interest in a security and— (a) the body corporate is, or its directors are, accustomed or under an obligation, whether formal or informal, to act in accordance with directions, instructions or wishes of that person in relation to that security; (b) that person has a controlling interest in the body corporate; or (c) that person is, or the associates of that person or that person and his associates are, entitled to exercise or control the exercise of not less than fifteen per cent of the votes attached to the voting shares in the body corporate.

A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security in any one or more of the following circumstances— (a) where he or she has entered into a contract to purchase a security; (b) where he or she has a right, otherwise than by reason of having an interest under a trust, to have a security transferred to himself or to his order, whether the right is exercisable presently or in the future and whether on the fulfillment of a condition or not; (c) where he or she has the right to acquire a security, or an interest in a security, under an option, whether on the fulfillment of a condition or not; or (d) where he or she is entitled, otherwise than by reason of his having been appointed a proxy or representative to vote at a meeting of members of a body corporate or of a class of its members, to exercise or control the exercise of a right attached to a security, not being a security of which he or she is the registered holder.
A person shall be deemed to have an interest in a security if that security is held jointly with another person.
For the purpose of determining whether a person has an interest in a security, it is immaterial that the interest cannot be related to a particular security.

There shall be disregarded— (a) an interest in a security if the interest is that of a person who holds the security as bare trustee; (b) an interest in a security of a person whose ordinary business includes the lending of money if he or she holds the interest only by way of security for the purposes of a transaction entered into in the ordinary course of business in connection with the lending of money; (c) an interest of a person in a security being an interest held by him or her by reason of his holding a prescribed office; and (d) a prescribed interest in a security being an interest of such person, or of the persons included in such class of persons as is prescribed.

An interest in a security shall not be disregarded by reason only of— (a) its remoteness; (b) the manner in which it arose; or (c) the fact that the exercise of a right conferred by the interest is, or is capable of being made, subject to restraint or restriction.

sections.source_quote: Definition of "interest in securities"

sections.heading: Establishment and membership of the Authority
sections.summary: This section establishes the Capital Markets Authority as a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal. It outlines the composition of the Authority, including the Chairperson appointed by the President on the Cabinet Secretary's recommendation, six other members appointed by the Cabinet Secretary, and specific roles such as the Principal Secretary to the National Treasury, the Governor of the Central Bank of Kenya, the Attorney-General, and the chief executive of the Authority. It also details the term of office, re-appointment eligibility, and conditions for resignation or removal.

requirements.text: There is hereby established an authority to be known as the Capital Markets Authority.

The Authority shall be a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal and shall be capable in its corporate name of— (a) suing and being sued; (b) taking, purchasing or otherwise acquiring, holding, charging and disposing of both movable and immovable property; (c) borrowing and lending money; (d) entering into contracts; and (e) doing or performing all such other things or acts necessary for the proper performance of its functions under this Act which may lawfully be done by a body corporate.

The Authority shall consist of— (a) a Chairperson to be appointed by the President on the recommendation of the Cabinet Secretary; (b) six other members appointed by the Cabinet Secretary; (c) the Principal Secretary to the National Treasury or a person deputed by him or her in writing for the purposes of this Act; (d) the Governor of the Central Bank of Kenya or a person deputed by him or her in writing for the purposes of this Act; (e) the Attorney-General or a person deputed by him or her in writing for the purposes of this Act; (f) the chief executive of the Authority.

The Chairperson and every member appointed under paragraph (b) of subsection (3) shall be appointed from amongst persons who have experience and expertise in legal, financial, banking, accounting, economics or insurance matters.

The Chairperson and every member appointed under paragraph (b) of subsection (3) shall hold office for a period of three years and shall be eligible for re-appointment for a further term of three years.
The members of the Authority shall be appointed at different times so that the respective expiry dates of their terms of office shall fall at different times.

Any member appointed under subsection (3)(b) shall cease to hold office if— (a) he or she delivers to the Cabinet Secretary a written resignation of his appointment; (b) on the advice of the Authority, the Cabinet Secretary removes him or her from office on the grounds that he or she is incapacitated by mental or physical illness or is otherwise unable or unfit to discharge the functions of a member or is unable to continue as a member; (c) he or she has been absent from three consecutive meetings of the Authority without leave or good cause; (d) he or she is adjudged bankrupt or enters into a composition scheme or arrangement with his creditors; (e) he or she is sentenced by a court to imprisonment for a term of six months or more; or (f) he or she is convicted of an offence involving dishonesty, fraud or moral turpitude.

In the event of vacation of office by any member appointed under subsection (3)(b) the Cabinet Secretary may appoint another person to hold office for the unexpired period of the term of office of the member in whose place he or she is appointed.

If any member of the Authority appointed under paragraph (b) of subsection (3) is temporarily unable to perform his duties, the Cabinet Secretary may appoint another person to act in his place during the period of his absence.
The members of the Authority shall be paid such remuneration and allowances out of the general fund of the Authority as may be determined by the Cabinet Secretary.

sections.source_quote: Establishment and membership of the Authority

sections.heading: Meetings and procedures of the Authority
sections.summary: The Authority's Board must meet at least six times annually with no more than two months between meetings. A quorum of six members, including the chief executive, is required for business decisions. The Chairperson presides at meetings but can be elected by members if absent. Decisions require a majority vote, with the Chairperson having a casting vote in case of equality. Temporary Chairperson appointments can be made during absence, and members with conflicts of interest must disclose their interests and recuse themselves from decisions unless the majority deems their expertise necessary.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes governance procedures for the Authority, ensuring regular board meetings and quorum requirements that may impact internal oversight mechanisms for data handling activities. The conflict of interest rules could affect data processing decisions involving internal stakeholders.

requirements.text: The Board shall meet not less than six times in every financial year and not more than two months shall elapse between the date of one meeting and the date of the next meeting.
The quorum for the conduct of the business of the Board shall be six members including the chief executive.

The Chairperson shall preside at every meeting of the Board at which he or she is present but in his absence, the members present shall elect one of their number who shall, with respect to that meeting and the business transacted thereat, have all the powers of the Chairperson.

All questions for decisions at any meeting of the Authority shall be decided by the vote of the majority of the members present and in case of an equality of votes the Chairperson shall have a casting vote.

If the Chairperson of the Authority, by reason of extended illness or absence is temporarily unable to perform the duties of his office, the President, on the recommendation of the Cabinet Secretary, shall appoint another member of the Authority to act in his place during the period of absence.
The Chairperson may at any time resign by a letter addressed to the President and the resignation shall take effect upon being accepted by the President.

Any member who has a direct or indirect interest in any decision that is to be taken on any specific non-rule making matter by the Authority, shall disclose the nature of such interest at the meeting of the Authority where such decision is being taken and the disclosure shall be recorded in the minutes of the meeting, and if either the member or majority of the members of the Authority believe that such member's interest in the matter is such as to influence his judgment, he or she shall not participate in the deliberation or the decision of the Authority on such matter.

Provided, that if a majority of the members in attendance at a meeting where such matter is considered determine that the experience or expertise of the interested member is necessary for the deliberation on the matter, they may permit such member to participate as they deem appropriate.

sections.source_quote: Meetings and procedures of the Authority (1) The Board shall meet not less than six times in every financial year and not more than two months shall elapse between the date of one meeting and the date of the next meeting.

sections.heading: Appointment of chief executive of the Authority
sections.summary: The Chief Executive of the Authority is appointed by the Cabinet Secretary with specific qualifications: at least ten years of senior management experience in law, finance, accounting, economics, banking, or insurance, and expertise in money or capital markets. The Chief Executive serves a four-year term with eligibility for reappointment for a further four years, but no more than two terms. The Chief Executive operates under the Authority's direction and is responsible for managing the Authority's affairs and functions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines the appointment and term of the Chief Executive, which may indirectly affect data governance structures within the Authority but does not directly address data transfer or processing requirements.

requirements.text: There shall be a Chief Executive of the Authority who shall be appointed by the Cabinet Secretary and who shall, subject to this section, hold office on such terms and conditions of service as may be specified in the instrument of appointment, or otherwise from time to time.

No person shall be qualified for appointment under this section unless such person— (a) has at least ten years' experience at a senior management level in matters relating to law, finance, accounting, economics, banking or insurance; and (b) has expertise in matters relating to money or capital markets or finance.
The Cabinet Secretary, in consultation with the Board, shall appoint a person qualified in terms of this section as the chief executive.

The chief executive shall hold office for a period of four years but shall be eligible for reappointment for a further term of four years: Provided that no person shall serve as the chief executive for more than two terms.

The chief executive shall, subject to the general direction and control of the Authority, be charged with the direction of the affairs and transactions of the Authority, the exercise, discharge and performance of its objectives, functions and duties, and the administration and control of the servants of the Authority.

sections.source_quote: Appointment of chief executive of the Authority (1) There shall be a Chief Executive of the Authority who shall be appointed by the Cabinet Secretary

sections.heading: Objectives of the Authority
sections.summary: The Authority aims to develop capital markets, facilitate securities and derivatives markets, ensure orderly trading, protect investor interests, establish compensation funds for broker failures, and promote electronic commerce for capital markets. It has broad powers to advise the government, implement policies, employ staff, impose sanctions, grant licenses, regulate markets, and conduct inspections. The Authority also ensures personal data processing complies with the Data Protection Act (Cap. 411C).
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines the Authority's objectives and powers, including specific obligations to ensure personal data processing aligns with the Data Protection Act (Cap. 411C), which directly impacts data handling practices within the capital markets sector.

requirements.text: The principal objectives of the Authority shall be— (a) the development of all aspects of the capital markets with particular emphasis on the removal of impediments to, and the creation of incentives for longer term investments in, productive enterprises; (b) to facilitate the existence of a nationwide system of securities commodities market and derivatives market and brokerage services so as to enable wider participation of the general public in the securities commodities market and derivatives market; (c) the creation, maintenance and regulation of a market in which securities can be issued and traded in an orderly, fair and efficient manner, through the implementation of a system in which the market participants are self-regulatory to the maximum practicable extent; (d) the protection of investor interests; (e) the facilitation of a compensation fund to protect investors from financial loss arising from the failure of a licensed broker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations; and (f) the development of a framework to facilitate the use of electronic commerce for the development of capital markets in Kenya.

A reference to electronic commerce shall be construed as a reference to the use of information technology to effect linkages among functions provided by licensed persons or other market participants and describes technology platforms that allow— (a) the transfer and dissemination of market information to a wider number of users within and between networks; (b) the offer, distribution or delivery in electronic form of securities or services ordinarily provided by licensed persons; and (c) the execution of securities transactions without the need for parties to the transaction to be physically present at the same location.

For the purpose of carrying out its objectives, the Authority may exercise, perform or discharge all or any of the following powers, duties and functions— (a) advise the Cabinet Secretary on all aspects of the development and operation of capital markets; (b) implement policies and programmes of the Government with respect to the capital markets; (c) employ such officers and servants as may be necessary for the proper discharge of the functions of the Authority; (cc) impose sanctions for breach of the provisions of this Act or the regulations made thereunder, or for non-compliance with the Authority's requirements or directions, and such sanctions may include— (i) levying of financial penalties, proportional to the gravity or severity of the breach, as may be prescribed; (ii) ordering a person to remedy or mitigate the effect of the breach, make restitution or pay compensation to any person aggrieved by the breach; (iii) publishing findings of malfeasance by any person; (iv) suspending or cancelling the listing of any securities or exchange-traded derivatives contracts, or the trading of any securities or exchange-traded derivatives contracts, for the protection of investors; (d) to issue guidelines and notices on all matters within the jurisdiction of the Authority under this Act; (e) to grant a licence to any person to operate as a stockbroker, derivatives broker, dealer or investment adviser, fund manager, investment bank, central depository or authorised securities dealer, and ensure the proper conduct of that business; (f) to grant approval to any person to operate as a securities exchange, commodity exchange, derivatives exchange, credit rating agency, registered venture capital company or to operate in any other capacity which directly contributes to the attainment of the objectives of this Act and to ensure the proper conduct of that business; (fa) regulate spot commodity markets; (ff) recognize any person duly licensed by a prescribed foreign authority to carry on any licensed activity in Kenya which requires a license or an approval under this Act; (g) register, approve and regulate collective investment schemes; (ga) license, approve and regulate private equity and venture capital companies that have access to public funds; (h) inquire, either on its own motion or at the request of any other person, into the affairs of any person which the Authority has approved or to which it has granted a licence and any public company the securities of which are publicly offered or traded on an approved securities exchange or on an over the counter market; (i) give directions to any person which the Authority has approved or to which it has granted a licence and any public company the securities of which are publicly offered or traded on an approved securities exchange or on an over the counter market; (j) conduct inspection of the activities, books and records of any persons approved or licensed by the Authority; (k) deleted by , s. 46(b); (l) deleted by , s. 46(b); (m) appoint an auditor to carry out a specific audit of the financial operations of any collective investment scheme or public company the securities of which are publicly offered or traded on an approved securities exchange or on an over the counter market, if such action is deemed to be in the interest of the investors, at the expense of such collective investment scheme or company; (n) grant compensation to any investor who suffers pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed broker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations; (o) have recourse against any person whose act or omission has resulted in a payment from the Compensation Fund; (p) act as an appellate body in respect of appeals against any self regulatory organization securities or exchange-traded derivatives contracts exchange, derivatives exchange or central depository in actions by parties aggrieved thereby; (q) co-operate or enter into agreements for mutual co-operation with other regulatory authorities for the development and regulation of cross-border activities in capital markets; (r) regulate and oversee the issue and subsequent trading, both in primary and secondary markets, of capital market instruments; (s) regulate the use of electronic commerce for dealing in securities or offer services ordinarily carried out by a licensed person; (t) trace any assets, including bank accounts, of any person who, upon investigation by the Authority, is found to have engaged in any fraudulent dealings in an issuer and its securities or insider trading; (u) in writing, order caveats to be placed against the title to such assets or prohibit any such person from operating any such bank accounts as may be directed by the Authority, pending determination of any charges instituted against that person; (v) prescribe notices or guidelines on corporate governance of a company whose securities have been issued to the public or a section of the public; (va) ensure processing of personal data in the operations of capital markets is in accordance with principles set out under the Data Protection Act (Cap. 411C); (w) do all such other acts as may be incidental or conducive to the attainment of the objectives of the Authority or the exercise of its powers under this Act.

sections.source_quote: Objectives of the Authority (1) The principal objectives of the Authority shall be— (a) the development of all aspects of the capital markets with particular emphasis on the removal of impediments to, and the creation of incentives for longer term investments in, productive enterprises; (b) to facilitate the existence of a nationwide system of securities commodities market and derivatives market and brokerage services so as to enable wider participation of the general public in the securities commodities market and derivatives market; (c) the creation, maintenance and regulation of a market in which securities can be issued and traded in an orderly, fair and efficient manner, through the implementation of a system in which the market participants are self-regulatory to the maximum practicable extent; (d) the protection of investor interests; (e) the facilitation of a compensation fund to protect investors from financial loss arising from the failure of a licensed broker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations; and (f) the development of a framework to facilitate the use of electronic commerce for the development of capital markets in Kenya.

sections.heading: Power of the Cabinet Secretary to issue rules and regulations
sections.summary: The Cabinet Secretary has authority to create rules and regulations covering securities listing, disclosures by market participants, record-keeping requirements, capital market operations, foreign investor participation, investment schemes, venture capital, credit rating agencies, securities trading, clearing, settlement, forex activities, fund managers, and financial penalties. These rules must align with promoting an efficient securities market and be publicly exposed for 30 days for stakeholder feedback.
sections.dtia_summary: Rules and regulations must be consistent with market efficiency objectives and undergo public consultation before implementation, which impacts how financial data is governed across borders through the Authority's regulatory framework.

requirements.text: The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate listing and de-listing of securities on a securities exchange
The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate disclosures about securities transactions by stockbrokers, derivatives brokers and dealers

The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate the keeping and proper maintenance of books, records, accounts and audits by all persons approved or licensed by the Authority
All rules and regulations formulated under subsection (1) shall take into account and be consistent with the objective of promoting and maintaining an effective and efficient securities market

All rules and regulations formulated under subsection (1) shall be exposed for comment by stakeholders and the general public for a period of thirty days through notification in at least two daily newspapers of national circulation and the electronic media

sections.source_quote: The Cabinet Secretary shall formulate such rules and regulations as may be required to regulate— (a) listing and de-listing of securities on a securities exchange; (b) disclosures about securities transactions by— (i) stockbrokers, derivatives brokers and dealers; (ii) persons who acquire or dispose of securities or exchange-traded derivatives contracts; (iii) a securities exchange;

sections.heading: Furnishing of information to the Authority
sections.summary: The Authority may require persons to provide information in writing within a specified period. The Authority may not disclose information obtained unless required by court law, except when assisting foreign regulatory bodies under specific conditions involving public interest or regulatory functions.
sections.dtia_summary: Information sharing with foreign regulators requires demonstrating public interest or regulatory function, which affects cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: The Authority or any person officially authorized in that behalf by the Authority may, by notice in writing, require any person to furnish to the Authority or to the authorized person, within such period as is specified in the notice, all such returns or information as specified in such notice

The Authority or any member thereof, or any officer or servant of the Authority, shall not disclose to any person or use any return or information acquired under subsection (1) except for the purpose of achieving the objectives of the Authority unless required to do so by a court of law

The Authority may, where it receives a request from a regulatory body, whether established within or outside Kenya, for assistance in investigating a person specified by the regulatory body who has contravened or is contravening any legal or regulatory requirements
A regulatory body which requests for assistance under subsection (3) shall demonstrate that it is desirable or expedient that the assistance requested should be provided in the interest of the public
A regulatory body which requests for assistance under subsection (3) shall demonstrate that the assistance shall assist the regulatory body in the discharge and performance of its functions

The Authority shall, in deciding whether the requirement under subsection (5) is satisfied in a particular case, take into account whether the regulatory body shall pay the Authority any of the costs and expenses incurred in providing the assistance

The Authority shall, in deciding whether the requirement under subsection (5) is satisfied in a particular case, take into account whether the regulatory body shall be able and willing to provide reciprocal assistance within its jurisdiction in response to a similar request for assistance from Kenya

sections.source_quote: The Authority or any person officially authorized in that behalf by the Authority may, by notice in writing, require any person to furnish to the Authority or to the authorized person, within such period as is specified in the notice, all such returns or information as specified in such notice.

sections.heading: Financial year of Authority
sections.summary: The Authority's financial year runs from July 1 to June 30 of the following year, defining the fiscal period for financial reporting and accountability.
sections.dtia_summary: The Authority's fiscal year structure impacts financial data retention and reporting timelines.

requirements.text: The financial year of the Authority shall be the period of twelve months commencing on the first day of July in each year

sections.source_quote: The financial year of the Authority shall be the period of twelve months commencing on the first day of July in each year.

sections.heading: Accounts
sections.summary: The Authority is required to maintain proper books of accounts detailing its income, expenditures, assets, liabilities, and all other transactions. This obligation ensures transparent financial record-keeping for the Authority's operations and accountability in financial management.

requirements.text: The Authority shall cause proper books of accounts to be kept of its income and expenditures, assets and liabilities and all other transactions of the Authority.

sections.source_quote: The Authority shall cause proper books of accounts to be kept of its income and expenditures, assets and liabilities and all other transactions of the Authority.

sections.heading: Establishment of the Investor Compensation Fund
sections.summary: This section establishes the Investor Compensation Fund to compensate investors who suffer pecuniary loss due to licensed stockbrokers or dealers failing to meet contractual obligations. The Fund is funded through contributions from licensed persons, fines, penalties, interest, recovered sums, and approved external sources. The Authority may reward individuals who provide timely information leading to Fund recoveries, with rewards capped at five million shillings. Offenders who collude or provide false information face fines up to five million shillings or imprisonment for five years. This section establishes the Investor Compensation Fund, requiring auditors to prepare and audit financial statements in accordance with regulatory standards. It mandates auditors to report specific adverse changes, potential contraventions of the Act, financial crimes, or serious irregularities to the Authority. The section explicitly states that such reports do not constitute auditor breaches of duty.
sections.dtia_summary: This section creates a financial safeguard mechanism for investor compensation but does not directly address data transfer or processing requirements. It is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments as it focuses on financial compensation mechanisms rather than data flows. This section is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments as it pertains to investor compensation funds and auditor reporting obligations unrelated to data transfers, international processing, or cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: There shall be established a Fund to be known as the Investor Compensation Fund for the purposes of granting compensation to investors who suffer pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed stockbroker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations.

The Compensation Fund shall consist of—(a) such moneys as are required to be paid into the Compensation Fund by licensed persons; (b) such sums of money as are paid under this Act as fines or penalties or under section 34 as ill-gotten gains where those harmed are not specifically identifiable; (c) such sums of money as accrue from interest and profits from investing Compensation Fund moneys; (d) such sums of money recovered by or on behalf of the Authority from entities whose failure to meet their obligations to investors result in payments from the Compensation Fund; (e) interest deemed to accrue on the proceeds of a public issue or offer for sale of shares of a company listed or to be listed on an approved securities exchange, between the closing date and the date of dispatch of refund cheques, or, where there is no refund, the date of dispatch of share certificates or crediting of securities accounts, to be determined at the rate prescribed by the Authority; (f) such sums of money as are received for purposes of the Compensation Fund from any other source approved by the Cabinet Secretary.

The Authority may reward any person who provides new and timely information leading to the recovery of sums of money referred to in subsection (2): Provided that—(a) this provision shall not apply to any officer of the Authority; (b) the reward payable under this subsection shall be three per cent of the amount recovered subject to a maximum of five million shillings; and (c) the reward referred to in paragraph (a) shall be paid before the recovered sums of money are transferred to the Fund.

A person who—(a) colludes with an officer of the Authority for the purpose of collecting the reward under subsection (2A); (b) while working at the Authority aids another person to get and provide information under subsection (2A); (c) provides false information under subsection (2A), commits an offence and shall on conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding five million shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years.
financial statements prepared and audited in accordance with the accounts and audit requirements for regulated persons
such other requirements as may be specified by the Authority

An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that there is or has been an adverse change in the risks inherent in the business of a self-regulatory organization with the potential to jeopardize the ability of the self-regulatory organization to continue as a going concern
An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that the self-regulatory organization may be in contravention of any provisions of this Act, or directions issued by the Authority
An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that a financial crime has been or is likely to be committed
An auditor who, in the course of his audit, has reason to believe that serious irregularities have occurred
shall report the matter, in writing, to the Authority

sections.source_quote: 18. Establishment of the Investor Compensation Fund (1) There shall be established a Fund to be known as the Investor Compensation Fund for the purposes of granting compensation to investors who suffer pecuniary loss resulting from the failure of a licensed stockbroker or dealer to meet his contractual obligations.

sections.heading: Application for securities exchange, commodities exchange approval
sections.summary: Applications for securities or commodities exchange approvals must be submitted to the Authority with prescribed fees and forms. The Authority may approve exchanges if they meet specific criteria, including being a limited liability company, having a properly constituted board, and adopting compliant rules. Approved exchanges must pay annual fees and comply with Authority requirements.

requirements.text: An application for securities exchange, commodities exchange or derivatives exchange approval shall be made to the Authority in the form and manner prescribed by the Authority and shall be accompanied by the prescribed fee.

The Authority may, by notice in writing, approve a person as a securities exchange, commodities exchange or derivatives exchange if it is satisfied that the applicant is a limited liability company whose liability is limited by shares, or as may be prescribed by the Authority.

The Authority may, by notice in writing, approve a person as a securities exchange, commodities exchange or derivatives exchange if it is satisfied that the applicant's board of directors is constituted in a manner prescribed by the Authority.

The Authority may, by notice in writing, approve a person as a securities exchange, commodities exchange or derivatives exchange if it is satisfied that the applicant has made and adopted rules in compliance with the Act and any Regulations made thereunder.

All fees to be charged by a securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange shall be subject to prior approval by the Authority notwithstanding the constitution of such securities exchange.

An approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange shall comply with all requirements of the Authority and pay an annual fee to the Authority at such rate as the Authority may prescribe.

The Authority may require an applicant for a licence as a securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange to lodge an application to be recognized as a self regulatory organization as a condition for obtaining and maintaining its licence.

sections.source_quote: An application for securities exchange, commodities exchange or derivatives exchange approval shall be made to the Authority in the form and manner prescribed by the Authority and shall be accompanied by the prescribed fee.

sections.heading: Changes in securities exchange, commodities exchange rules
sections.summary: Approved exchanges must seek prior Authority approval before amending their rules. The Authority reviews proposed rule changes within 30 days, providing reasons for disallowance if applicable. Certain rule changes may take effect immediately if designated as policy, fee adjustments, or administrative matters. Exchanges must inform the Authority of any changes to previously submitted information.

requirements.text: The rules of an approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange, in so far as they have been approved by the Authority, shall not be amended, varied or rescinded without the prior approval of the Authority.

Where the board of directors of an approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange wishes to amend its rules, it shall forward the amendments to the Authority for approval.

The Authority shall, after hearing from the securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange, and within thirty days of receipt of a notice under subsection (2) give written notice to the securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange stating whether such amendments to the rules are allowed or disallowed and in the event of the rules being disallowed, the Authority shall give reasons for such disallowance.

Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2), a proposed rule change may take effect upon filing with the Authority if designated by the exchanges as a stated policy, practice or interpretation with respect to the meaning, administration or enforcement of an existing rule.

Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2), a proposed rule change may take effect upon filing with the Authority if designated by the exchanges as a proposal establishing or changing a fee or other charge.

Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2), a proposed rule change may take effect upon filing with the Authority if designated by the exchanges as a proposal dealing solely with the administration of the exchange or other matters which the Authority may specify.

Where an approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange proposes to alter any particulars already furnished or undergoes or intends to undergo a change from its state specified in the application for approval it shall inform the Authority and obtain its prior consent before such alteration or change is effected.

sections.source_quote: The rules of an approved securities exchange, commodities exchange or a derivatives exchange, in so far as they have been approved by the Authority, shall not be amended, varied or rescinded without the prior approval of the Authority.

sections.heading: Disciplinary action by securities exchange
sections.summary: A securities exchange or derivatives exchange must notify the Authority within seven days when taking disciplinary action against a trading participant or listed company. The Authority may review such actions, affirm or set aside decisions after providing opportunities for hearing. The Authority can also suspend or expel individuals directly if an exchange fails to act, following similar hearing procedures.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires exchanges to report disciplinary actions to the regulator, enabling oversight of cross-border compliance. The regulator's power to review and override exchange decisions ensures accountability in disciplinary processes, which impacts data transfer assessments involving exchange participants.

requirements.text: A securities exchange or derivatives exchange must notify the Authority within seven days when taking disciplinary action against a trading participant or listed company.
The Authority may review disciplinary actions taken by exchanges and affirm or set aside decisions after providing opportunities for hearing.
The Authority can suspend or expel individuals directly if an exchange fails to act, following similar hearing procedures.

sections.source_quote: Where a securities exchange or a derivatives exchange reprimands, fines, suspends or expels, or otherwise takes disciplinary action against a trading participant or a listed company, it shall within seven days give notice to the Authority in writing, giving particulars including the name of the person, the reason for and nature of the action taken.

sections.heading: Licences required
sections.summary: Businesses must hold valid licenses from the Authority to operate as stockbrokers, dealers, investment advisors, or other financial entities. Exchanges and other entities must be approved by the Authority, and license holders must meet financial and other requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing requirements for financial entities, which impacts data transfer assessments by ensuring regulated entities handle data under defined oversight frameworks.

requirements.text: No person may operate as a stockbroker, derivatives broker, or other financial entity without a valid license from the Authority.
Exchanges and other entities must be approved by the Authority.
License holders must meet minimum financial and other requirements.

sections.source_quote: No person shall carry on business as a stockbroker, derivatives broker, REIT manager, trustee, dealer, investment adviser, fund manager, investment bank, central depository, authorised securities dealer, authorized depository, online forex broker, commodity dealer, commodity broker or hold himself out as carrying on such a business unless he or she holds a valid licence issued under this Act or under the authority of this Act.

sections.heading: Application for licence
sections.summary: Applications for licenses must be submitted to the Authority with prescribed forms and fees. The Authority may require additional information, assess financial requirements, impose conditions, and provide opportunities for hearing before granting licenses.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines the licensing process, which impacts data transfer assessments by ensuring that entities handling data are properly licensed and compliant with regulatory requirements.

requirements.text: Applications for licenses must be submitted to the Authority with prescribed forms and fees.
The Authority may require additional information from applicants.
License holders must meet minimum financial and other requirements.
The Authority may impose conditions or restrictions on licenses.
The Authority must provide an opportunity for hearing before granting licenses.

sections.source_quote: An application for a licence or for the renewal of a licence shall be made to the Authority in the prescribed form and shall be accompanied by the prescribed fee and in the case of an application for the renewal of a licence, may be made within three months but not later than one month prior to the expiry of the licence.

sections.heading: [Repealed by , s. 18.]
sections.summary: The Authority may impose sanctions including public reprimands, trading suspensions, license revocations, financial penalties, and restitution orders for breaches of the Act. Penalties are calculated based on the benefit accrued from breaches.
sections.dtia_summary: This section details regulatory sanctions for breaches, which impacts data transfer assessments by imposing financial and operational consequences for non-compliance with data handling practices.

requirements.text: The Authority may impose public reprimands, trading suspensions, license revocations, financial penalties, and restitution orders for breaches of the Act.
Financial penalties for breaches may be up to ten million shillings for licensed persons.
Restitution orders may be imposed for losses to aggrieved persons, with quantified and proven losses.

sections.source_quote: Without prejudice to any other provision of this Act, the Authority may impose the following sanctions or levy financial penalties in accordance with this Act, for the breach of any provisions of this Act, the regulations, rules, guidelines, notices or directions made thereunder, or the rules of procedure of a securities, commodities or derivatives exchange, by a licensed or approved person, issuer, employee or a director of a licensed or approved person or employee director of a issuer as provided under section 11(3)(cc)

sections.heading: Suspension or revocation of a licence
sections.summary: The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who goes into liquidation, engages in unauthorized activities, has a receiver appointed, ceases business without approval, has directors not performing duties honestly, fails to comply with license conditions, fails to follow directions, fails to provide information, provides false information, or is no longer fit to hold a license. Suspension cannot exceed three months and may be extended once. Revocation occurs if annual fees are unpaid within thirty days after suspension. The Authority must publish suspended or revoked licenses in the Gazette and provide an opportunity to be heard.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines conditions under which a license can be suspended or revoked, impacting data handling by licensed entities. It requires the Authority to publish actions and provide hearing opportunities, which may affect data transfer compliance if licenses are revoked.

requirements.text: The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who goes into liquidation or has an order issued for winding up
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who carries out activities outside the scope of licensed or approved activities
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who has a receiver or manager appointed on all or substantial part of the company's property
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who ceases business without approval after thirty days
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person whose directors or key employees have not performed duties honestly and fairly
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who has contravened license conditions
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who fails to comply with Authority directions
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who fails to provide required information
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who provides false or misleading information
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who is no longer fit to hold a license
The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for a licensed person who breaches other provisions of the Act
Suspension of a license shall not exceed three months and may be extended for a further period not exceeding three months
The Authority shall lift suspension or revoke license at expiry of suspension period
Failure to pay annual fee results in license suspension
If suspension occurs and fee not paid within thirty days, license is revoked
The Authority may revoke or suspend a license at request of licensed person
The Authority shall publish suspended or revoked licenses in the Gazette
The Authority shall provide opportunity to be heard before taking action under sections 25 and 26

sections.source_quote: The Authority may suspend or revoke a license for such period or until the occurrence of such event as the Authority may specify, if a licensed person—(a) goes into liquidation or an order is issued for the winding up of the licensed person; (b) carries out any activity outside the scope of the licensed or approved activities; (c) has a receiver or a manager appointed on all or a substantial part of the property of the company; (d) ceases to carry on the licensed business for a period of more than thirty days unless it has obtained the approval of the Authority to do so; (e) any of its directors or key employees has not, in the opinion of the Authority, performed their duties honestly and fairly; (f) has contravened or failed to comply with any condition applicable in respect of the licence; (g) fails to comply with a direction of the Authority; (h) fails to provide the Authority with such information as it may require; (i) provides false or misleading information; (j) for any other reason, is no longer fit and proper person to hold a license; or (k) is in breach of any other provision under this Act.

sections.heading: Obligation to report changes
sections.summary: A licensed person must report to the Authority within fourteen days if they cease business or if there is a change in particulars required to be entered in the register of license holders.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires timely reporting of business cessation or changes to license holder details, which may impact data transfer compliance if the licensed entity's data handling practices change.

requirements.text: A licensed person must report to the Authority within fourteen days if they cease business
A licensed person must report to the Authority within fourteen days if there is a change in particulars required to be entered in the register of license holders

sections.source_quote: Where—(a) the holder of a licence ceases to carry on the business to which the licence relates; or (b) a change occurs in any particulars which are required by section 27 to be entered in a register of licence holders with respect to the holder of a licence, the holder of the licence shall within fourteen days of the occurrence of the event concerned, give to the Authority, particulars of such event.

sections.heading: Licensing requirements
sections.summary: Before granting a license, the Authority must verify that the applicant is a legally constituted entity, has qualified directors, meets administrative capacity requirements, and for specific licenses (stockbrokers, dealers, derivatives brokers) has security deposits, operates solely for clients, or meets other conditions. The Authority also ensures that no person controls more than 33.3% of a company's shares or voting rights, and that key personnel do not hold excessive control.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes licensing requirements that affect data handling by financial entities, including verification of entity structure, director qualifications, and restrictions on shareholding and control, which may impact data transfer compliance.

requirements.text: The Authority must verify that the applicant is a legally constituted entity or a collective investment scheme
The Authority must verify that at least one director, chief executive officer, or person directing the business has satisfied minimum qualification requirements
For stockbrokers, dealers, or other persons, the applicant company must have lodged security or equivalent bank guarantee with the securities exchange or Authority
The applicant company must have necessary administrative capacity to carry on business
For stockbroker licenses, the applicant must carry on business solely on behalf of clients
For dealer licenses, the applicant must carry on business solely on their own behalf
For derivatives broker licenses, the applicant may carry on business either on behalf of clients or on their own behalf, or both
A securities exchange or derivatives exchange may admit an applicant if they fulfill all requirements imposed by the Authority and relevant exchange, and pay an admission fee
A securities broker, derivatives broker, or dealer whose license is revoked under section 26 shall cease to be a trading participant of the securities exchange
No individual or corporate person may control or be beneficially entitled to more than 33.3% of issued share capital or voting rights in a company
No individual or corporate person may appoint more than one-third of the members of the Board of directors
No individual or corporate person may receive more than 33.3% of aggregate dividends and interest on shareholders loans
The provisions of subsection (4) do not apply to corporate entities licensed by banking, insurance, pensions or securities regulators in Kenya

The provisions of subsection (4) do not apply where ownership structure is diverse and no person holds or controls more than 25% of shares, votes, directorship appointments, dividends or interest on shareholder loans
No person who exercises control or is beneficially entitled to more than 25% of listed share capital or voting rights may be appointed as key personnel

sections.source_quote: Before granting any licence or approval, the Authority in respect of a business that requires to be licensed or approved shall satisfy itself—(a) that the applicant is such legal entity as may be prescribed in the Regulations as the Authority may prescribe or is duly constituted as a collective investment scheme; (b) that at least one the director, chief executive officer or such other person who directs, conducts, manages or supervises the business of the applicant has satisfied such minimum qualification requirements as may be prescribed; (c) in the case of a stockbroker, dealer or other person prescribed by the Authority that the applicant company has lodged security in such sum as may be determined by the Authority or an equivalent bank guarantee or bond with the securities exchange in which it is a trading participant or with the Authority or other person approved by the Authority as the case may be; (d) that the applicant company has the necessary administrative capacity to carry on business for which the licence is required; (e) in the case of an application for a stockbroker's licence, that the applicant shall carry on business solely on behalf of clients; (f) in the case of an application for a dealer's licence, that the applicant shall carry on business solely on the applicant's own behalf; (gg) in the case of an application for a derivatives broker licences, that the applicant may carry on business either on behalf of clients or on the applicant's own behalf, or both; (h) deleted by , s. 7.

sections.heading: Procedure for collective investment schemes
sections.summary: [extraction failed: model output unparseable] This section outlines the procedural requirements for collective investment schemes involving asset-backed securities. It specifies that assets must be legally originated, sold, transferred, or assigned, and comply with the Act. The section details the origination of assets into securitisation trusts, the requirement for true sales according to Kenyan law, and the legal nature of transfers to avoid equitable treatment. It also mandates that failures to achieve true sales do not invalidate securities offerings or adversely affect investor rights. This section outlines the procedural requirements for collective investment schemes involving restricted offers, including the authority's power to apply court procedures for unfair prejudice and require records in cases of restricted offers. It specifies that these procedures mirror those for listed asset-backed securities.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes conditions for asset-backed securities transactions to ensure legal transfers and true sales, which are critical for DTIA assessments to verify compliance with cross-border transfer mechanisms and trust structures in securitisation.

requirements.text: Assets must be capable of being legally originated, sold, transferred or assigned
Assets must comply with any requirements imposed under this Act

All securitisation transactions shall involve either direct origination of assets into the securitisation trust or sale, transfer or assignment of assets to the trustee in a true sale according to Kenyan law
The sale, transfer or assignment must be a legal and not an equitable sale
Failure to achieve a true sale shall not invalidate the sale, transfer or assignment, the issue or offer of asset backed securities, or adversely affect investor rights

The powers of the Authority to make an application to court in case of unfair prejudice under section 33C; or require production of records and documents under section 33D shall apply to restricted offers in the same manner as if the asset backed securities had been listed.

sections.source_quote: a third party at the time at which an issue or offer of asset backed securities is made; be capable of being legally originated, sold, transferred or assigned; and comply with any requirements imposed under this Act.

sections.heading: Transactions in securities
sections.summary: Licensed persons, brokers, and dealers may not transfer listed securities outside the securities exchange without Authority approval and payment of a fee. The Authority may authorize transfers for private transactions, over-the-counter trades, or in the interest of shareholders. No licensed person may trade in securities contrary to Authority rules, effect transactions in margin accounts contrary to Authority requirements, lend securities without customer consent, or engage in manipulative deception or fraud.
sections.dtia_summary: This section restricts securities transfers and transactions, requiring Authority approval for cross-border or external transfers, which directly impacts data transfer compliance for financial entities handling securities data.

requirements.text: No licensed person, broker or dealer shall transfer listed securities outside the securities exchange without Authority approval and payment of a prescribed fee
