The Authority may authorize transfer of listed security outside the securities exchange for private transactions, over-the-counter trades, or in the interest of shareholders
No licensed person, broker or dealer shall trade in listed securities in contravention of Authority-prescribed rules on clearance, settlement, payment, transfer or delivery
No licensed person, broker or dealer shall effect transactions in margin accounts contrary to Authority requirements
No licensed person, broker or dealer shall lend or arrange for lending securities without customer's written consent
No licensed person, broker or dealer shall borrow or arrange to borrow securities as collateral without customer's written consent
No licensed person, broker or dealer shall effect transactions in, or induce purchase or sale of listed securities by means of manipulative deception or fraudulent devices
No person holding shares in a public company listed on an approved securities exchange shall sell or transfer shares except in compliance with exchange trading procedures

No person shall employ devices, schemes or artifices to defraud, engage in acts that operate as fraud or deceit, make untrue statements of material facts, or omit material facts necessary to avoid misleading statements

sections.source_quote: No licensed person, broker or dealer shall transfer listed securities outside the securities exchange in which he or she is a trading participant except as provided for by the Authority in rules or as authorised by the Authority on a case by case basis, and on payment of a prescribed fee.

sections.heading: Register of interest in securities
sections.summary: [extraction failed: model output unparseable] This section imposes penalties for unauthorized gains or losses from securities transactions. A licensed entity that engages in such activities faces a fine up to ten million shillings and must pay twice the amount of the gain or loss avoided. The penalty applies to entities that contravene the Act's provisions regarding securities transactions. This section mandates that the Authority may issue directions requiring companies or persons to produce specific records and documents related to misconduct, inadequate information disclosure, or other specified matters. It outlines the Authority's power to authorize individuals to compel production of records, and specifies that banks are limited to making copies or extracts of documents. The section also establishes that contravening these requirements constitutes an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIAs by requiring detailed record production for investigations into misconduct or information gaps, with specific limitations on bank records. It may necessitate data sharing with regulators for compliance checks.

requirements.text: A fine not exceeding ten million shillings and payment of twice the amount of the gain made or loss avoided
The Authority may issue directions requiring a company or person to produce records and documents related to misconduct, inadequate information disclosure, or other specified matters
The Authority may authorize a person to require a company or other person to produce records and documents specified by the authorized person
The Authority or authorized person may require a person in possession of records to produce them to the Authority or authorized person
The power to require records or documents includes taking copies or extracts, demanding explanations from officers or employees, or requiring explanations for failure to produce records
For banks, the power is limited to making copies or extracts of documents
Contravening this section constitutes an offence

sections.source_quote: company, to a fine not exceeding ten million shillings and payment of twice the amount of the gain made or loss avoided.

sections.heading: Other offences
sections.summary: Committing an offence under this Act includes contravening provisions of the Act, furnishing false information, or wilfully obstructing Authority members. Offences may be punished with fines and imprisonment, with penalties recoverable as civil debts. Compensation may be ordered for losses caused by the offence, and the Authority may compound offences with payment of a portion of the maximum fine.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines offences related to financial misconduct, including false information and obstruction of Authority, which may impact data transfer compliance if licensed entities provide inaccurate data.

requirements.text: Any person who contravenes provisions of this Act or any requirement imposed under this Act or rules/regulations made thereunder commits an offence
Any person who furnishes or publishes information that is untrue or incorrect or misleading because of material omissions commits an offence
Any person who wilfully obstructs Authority members in the performance of duties under this Act commits an offence
On first offence, an individual may be fined up to five million shillings or imprisoned for up to two years and pay two times the gain made or loss avoided
On subsequent offence, an individual may be fined up to ten million shillings or imprisoned for up to five years and pay three times the gain made or loss avoided
On first offence, a company may be fined up to ten million shillings and pay two times the gain made or loss avoided
On subsequent offence, a company may be fined up to thirty million shillings and pay three times the gain made or loss avoided
Financial penalties imposed under this section shall be recoverable summarily by the Authority as civil debts
The court may order compensation to a person who suffers loss by reason of the offence
Compensation orders may be in addition to or in substitution of other penalties
The amount of restitution or compensation is the loss sustained, profits accrued, or illegal gains received or loss averted
The Authority may compound an offence with payment of up to two-thirds of the maximum fine

sections.source_quote: Any person who—(a) contravenes any provision of this Act or any requirement imposed under the provision of this Act or any rule or regulation made thereunder; (b) furnishes or publishes for the purpose of this Act or in connection with an issuer whose securities are listed or quoted to be listed on a securities exchange, or issued or to be issued to the public or a collective investment scheme, any information or any returns the contents of which are to his knowledge untrue or incorrect or misleading because of material omissions; or (c) wilfully obstructs any member of the Authority or an officer or servant of the Authority in the performance of his duties under the provisions of that Act, shall be guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Appeals from action by Authority
sections.summary: Any person aggrieved by a direction or decision from the Authority regarding licence refusal, limitations, suspension, revocation, public offer approval, securities listing, trading suspension, or compensation refusal may appeal to the Capital Markets Tribunal within fifteen days. The Tribunal may require the Authority or Investor Compensation Fund Board to show cause, affirm or set aside decisions after hearing, and must issue awards within ninety days. The Tribunal has powers similar to the High Court for evidence and proceedings.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes an appeals mechanism for financial entities affected by Authority decisions, but does not directly impact data transfers or international data flows. It is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments.

requirements.text: Any person aggrieved by any direction given by the Authority to such person or by a decision of the Authority or by the Investor Compensation Fund Board—refusing to grant a licence; imposing limitations or restrictions on a licence; suspending or revoking a licence; refusing to approve a public offer of securities; refusing to admit a security to the official list of a securities exchange; suspending trading of a security on a securities exchange; or requiring the removal of a security from the official list of a securities exchange—may appeal to the Capital Markets Tribunal against such directions, refusal, limitations or restrictions, cancellations, suspension or removal, as the case may be, within fifteen days from the date on which the decision was communicated to such person.

The Capital Markets Tribunal may require the Authority or the Investor Compensation Fund Board to show cause for its action or decision, and may affirm or, after affording the Authority or the Board an opportunity to be heard, set aside such action or decision.
The Tribunal shall hear and determine an appeal within ninety days from the date of filing of the appeal.

sections.source_quote: Any person aggrieved by any direction given by the Authority to such person or by a decision of the Authority or by the Investor Compensation Fund Board—refusing to grant a licence; imposing limitations or restrictions on a licence; suspending or revoking a licence; refusing to approve a public offer of securities; refusing to admit a security to the official list of a securities exchange; suspending trading of a security on a securities exchange; or requiring the removal of a security from the official list of a securities exchange—may appeal to the Capital Markets Tribunal against such directions, refusal, limitations or restrictions, cancellations, suspension or removal, as the case may be, within fifteen days from the date on which the decision was communicated to such person.

sections.heading: Directions and submission of reports
sections.summary: The Cabinet Secretary may direct the Authority to provide returns, accounts, and information on its work. The Authority must submit annual operational reports and audited accounts to the Cabinet Secretary within six months of the financial year-end, which are then tabled in Parliament within three months.
sections.dtia_summary: This section governs reporting obligations for financial entities but does not directly impact data transfers or international data flows. It is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments.

requirements.text: The Cabinet Secretary may, from time to time, direct the Authority to furnish in such form as he or she may require, returns, accounts and any other information with respect to the work of the Authority and the Authority shall comply with such direction.

The Authority shall, within six months after the close of each financial year, submit to the Cabinet Secretary a report of its operations and activities throughout the year together with audited accounts in such form and detail as the Cabinet Secretary shall, from time to time, determine.
The Cabinet Secretary shall table the report submitted under subsection (3) before above to Parliament within three months of its submission.

sections.source_quote: The Authority shall, within six months after the close of each financial year, submit to the Cabinet Secretary a report of its operations and activities throughout the year together with audited accounts in such form and detail as the Cabinet Secretary shall, from time to time, determine.
